data.body:
PREPLANNING_CONTEXT

Epic: 81b4-c50c — Hierarchy-Aware Dependency Enforcement for the Ticket System
Escalation policy: Escalate when blocked — agents proceed unless a significant assumption is required that could send implementation in the wrong direction; all assumptions documented.

Story Map (5 stories, serial chain):
  S1: 3f65-65ee — resolve_hierarchy_link() library (walking skeleton) [priority 1]
  S2: 983e-7fff — add_dependency() integration: promote/reject cross-boundary links [priority 1, depends S1]
  S3: b123-2e92 — check_cycle_at_level(): circular + self-referential detection [priority 1, depends S2]
  S4: f813-af53 — Performance benchmark + dep-graph e2e test [priority 2, depends S3]
  S5: d80c-dd3d — Documentation update (CLI reference + CLAUDE.md) [priority 3, depends S1-S4]

Key architectural decisions:
- Integration point: add_dependency() in ticket-graph.py (NOT ticket-link.sh — shim routes 'ticket link' to python3 ticket-graph.py --link)
- Parent_id lookups: must use _reduce_ticket_state() — ticket-graph.py explicitly skips .cache.json
- Hierarchy levels: epic (no parent_id) → story (parent_id=epic) → task (parent_id=story)
- Divergence-point algorithm: walk parent_id chain ≤2 hops per ticket, find where chains diverge, redirect to that level
- Cycle detection scope: S3 must decide whether check_cycle_at_level() subsumes existing check_would_create_cycle() call
- Out of scope: 'ticket unlink' path for promoted links (documented known limitation)
- Performance target: <5s on 1,000-ticket tree (SC13)

Risk register flags applied as story considerations:
- Testing: new cycle detection logic — ensure mock-compatible interface for unit tests
- Performance: parent-chain traversal must remain O(1) regardless of total ticket count
- Reliability: flock serialization in add_dependency() must be preserved
- Security: no user-supplied values interpolated into event file paths
